document.source_title: Operationalizing Economic Statecraft: A New Imperative for the Pentagon
document.author: Anita R. Kellogg, Ph.D.
document.summary: The article discusses the necessity for the Pentagon to integrate economic statecraft into its military strategy, emphasizing the importance of supply chains in modern warfare. It highlights how adversaries leverage economic tools like sanctions and export controls to gain military advantages. The author advocates for the establishment of an Economic Warfare Operations Capability within the Pentagon to better coordinate efforts and enhance supply chain resilience.
key_takeaways: Economic statecraft is becoming a primary instrument of competition in warfare.
Supply chain vulnerabilities pose significant risks to military operations.
The Pentagon needs to incorporate economic tools into operational planning.
A dedicated entity, such as the Economic Warfare Operations Capability, is essential for coordinating economic statecraft efforts.
Trust and cooperation between the private sector and the Pentagon are crucial for effective economic statecraft.
Education and expertise in economic statecraft are lacking among military leaders.
document.why_it_matters: The integration of economic statecraft into military strategy is critical for the U.S. to maintain its competitive edge and ensure national security in an era where adversaries exploit economic vulnerabilities.

claims.claim_text: The next major war could be decided by supply chains before combat begins.
claims.evidence_text: The next major war the United States fights could be decided by supply chains long before the first shot is fired.

claims.claim_text: China has enacted strict export restrictions on drones bound for Ukraine.
claims.evidence_text: China has enacted strict export restrictions on drones and drone parts bound for Ukraine, directly degrading its military capability.

claims.claim_text: The Pentagon lacks a central coordinating body for economic statecraft.
claims.evidence_text: What is missing is an integrating body that aligns these efforts toward common objectives.

entities.name: Economic Warfare Operations Capability
entities.description: A proposed entity within the Pentagon to coordinate economic statecraft efforts.
entities.evidence_text: That is precisely the role the Economic Warfare Operations Capability could play.

entities.name: Potomac Institute for Policy Studies
entities.description: An organization involved in the series examining economic statecraft.
entities.evidence_text: This special series is brought to you by the Potomac Institute for Policy Studies and War on the Rocks.

entities.name: War on the Rocks

entities.evidence_text: This special series is brought to you by the Potomac Institute for Policy Studies and War on the Rocks.

metrics.name: Visibility into tier-one suppliers
metrics.evidence_text: Recent surveys show that although 60 percent of firms report comprehensive visibility into tier-one suppliers.

metrics.name: Visibility into suppliers beyond tier-one
metrics.evidence_text: Only 30 percent report visibility further downstream.

metrics.name: Visibility into tier-four suppliers and beyond
metrics.evidence_text: Only 17.1 percent analyze critical suppliers down to tier 4 and beyond.
